mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling for managing the lifecycle of Kubernetes clusters. It simplifies the provisioning, upgrading, and operation of multiple clusters across various infrastructure environments. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

```

> This is the official repository for Cluster API charts.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) required for Cluster API to function.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

> This YAML file contains all necessary CRDs.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API controller
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API controller using Helm, which manages the lifecycle of clusters.

```bash

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

```

> Make sure to install in the kube-system namespace.

steps.title: Create a Cluster API custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a Cluster API custom resource to define a new cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-cluster-infra
EOF

```

> Replace 'MyInfrastructureProviderCluster' with your actual infrastructure provider.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-cluster-infra
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Cluster API installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Cluster API components are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> You should see the Cluster API controller pods running.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API
uninstall.description:
This step removes Cluster API and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all managed clusters and their data.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace kube-system

kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml

```

> This command removes the Cluster API controller.
> This command removes the CRDs.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your cluster resources. After upgrading, verify that the components are functioning correctly.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -o yaml > cluster-backup.yaml

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

kubectl get pods -n kube-system

```

> Backup existing cluster resources.
> This command upgrades the Cluster API controller.
> Verify that the upgraded pods are running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unable to connect to the server: dial tcp <ip>:<port>: connect: connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Cluster API controller is not running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Cluster API controller pod using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n kube-system'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Cluster" in version "cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The CRDs for Cluster API are not installed or are outdated.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRDs using 'kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api-components.yaml'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to create cluster: infrastructure provider not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified infrastructure provider is not installed or configured.

**Fix:**
Ensure the infrastructure provider CRDs and controllers are installed and configured correctly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'context deadline exceeded'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Cluster API controller is taking too long to respond, possibly due to resource constraints.

**Fix:**
Check the resource allocation for the Cluster API controller and increase if necessary.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new infrastructure providers, improved error handling in controllers.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the old v1alpha1 API versions for Cluster and Machine resources.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.11.x, ensure to migrate your resources to the new v1beta1 API.

resolution.summary: You should see the output of 'kubectl get clusters' showing your clusters with their statuses.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-cluster-infra
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the specified resources.
